abstract: As scientific literature and technology evolve at an unprecedented pace, scholars are increasingly turning to advanced artificial intelligence models to streamline research and improve information accessibility. The emergence of OpenAI’s ChatGPT represents a transformative milestone in large language model (LLM) technology, fundamentally altering the landscape of AI. Since its debut, various sectors have explored ChatGPT’s utility, focusing on its sophisticated natural language processing and conversational capabilities. This article investigates the critical intersection of generative AI and governance, asking to what extent ChatGPT can influence public policy analysis. The researcher evaluates the model’s potential to enhance data-driven decision-making while addressing the persistent challenges—such as algorithmic transparency, inherent bias, and contextual limitations—that researchers and policymakers must navigate.
